chunk_summary: This section outlines clinic-ready strategies for assessing adolescent nutrition, emphasizing structured evaluations, anthropometry, and resource-adapted baseline investigations tailored to severity and local guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand structured clinic assessments for adolescent nutrition, including dietary recall and anthropometry.
Identify appropriate baseline investigations based on severity and resource availability.
Apply adolescent-specific growth references like IAP 2015 and WHO 2007 for anthropometric evaluation.
key_facts: IAP 2015 growth charts are widely used in Indian clinics for ages 5–18 years.
WHO 2007 defines thinness as BMI-for-age <-2 SD, overweight >+1 SD, and obesity >+2 SD.
Baseline investigations include CBC, ferritin, TSH, and 25(OH)D, with additional tests for overweight/obesity like fasting lipids, HbA1c, and ALT.
MASLD screening is recommended for adolescents ≥10 years with overweight/obesity.
Electrolytes, LFT, ECG, and micronutrient testing are advised for higher physiologic risk cases.
reference_quote: WHO 2007 reference defines thinness as BMI-for-age <-2 SD, overweight >+1 SD, and obesity >+2 SD. (Source: WHO growth reference data for 5–19 years)
document_key_topics: Baseline investigations for nutritional and metabolic health
